title: NIS2 Effectiveness Assessment of Cybersecurity Measures

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing: SPL directly addresses Art.21(2)(f) requirements with specific, actionable monitoring.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) (Policies and procedures effectiveness) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.9: NIS2 Effectiveness Assessment of Cybersecurity Measures.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.9' running on index=risk, index=vulnerability, index=itsi_summary, _audit, _internal, CIM data models, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:risk` carries `risk_id`, `treatment_status`, `review_due`, `risk_owner`; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` carries `policy_number`, `approval_status`, `next_review_date` (Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, Splunkbase 1928). `index=vuln` `sourcetype=tenable:vuln` includes `severity`, `first_found`, `state`; `index=pentest` `sourcetype=pentest:finding` includes `finding_id`, `remediation_due`. MFA coverage proxy uses `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunk_web_access` with `user`. Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) and Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841) contextualise service outcomes via `index=itsi_summary` fields `health_score`, `service_name`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060)
spl:
| makeresults
| eval measure="MFA_Coverage"
| append [
    search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_web_access earliest=-24h
    | stats dc(user) as total_users dc(eval(if(match(_raw,"(?i)mfa|2fa|totp"),user,null()))) as mfa_users
    | eval measure="MFA_Coverage", pct=round(100*mfa_users/total_users,1), target=95
]
| append [
    search index=vuln sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active" earliest=-30d
    | eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, 1=1,90)
    | eval age_days=round((now()-first_found)/86400,1)
    | eval in_sla=if(age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
    | stats avg(in_sla) as pct_raw
    | eval measure="Patch_SLA_Compliance", pct=round(pct_raw*100,1), target=90
]
| append [
    search index=itsi_summary is_service_in_maintenance=0 earliest=-7d
    | stats avg(eval(if(health_score>=70,1,0))) as pct_raw by service_name
    | stats avg(pct_raw) as pct_raw
    | eval measure="Service_Availability", pct=round(pct_raw*100,1), target=99
]
| where isnotnull(pct)
| eval status=if(pct>=target,"PASS","FAIL")
| table measure, pct, target, status
description: Rolls up measurable signals—login hardening proxy from web UI patterns, open vulnerability remediation pace against tiered SLAs, and IT service health scores—into pass/fail KPI tiles so teams can show that security controls are working in practice, not only documented.
value: Cuts audit prep time by giving risk and compliance leaders one scheduled export that ties everyday operations (patch queues, service health, workforce sign-in posture) to the effectiveness story regulators expect, with timestamps suitable for long-term evidence stores.
implementation: (1) Confirm `index=vuln` and `index=itsi_summary` are populated; map Tenable fields per TA (Splunkbase 4060). (2) If grc:risk or snow:sn_grc_policy feeds exist (Splunkbase 1928), add a second dashboard panel for treatment-age—not this base SPL. (3) Save as `UC-22.2.9` with cron monthly plus on-demand for board packs. (4) Route FAIL rows to the CISO office channel; throttle per `measure`. (5) Archive CSV/PDF outputs to `index=audit_evidence` with signing per `evidenceSigning`.
visualization: KPI tiles (pass/fail per measure), gauges (% vs target), sortable table of failing measures, line chart of rolling quarter trends.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Splunk Web MFA keyword noise** — Distinguish: help-desk articles or marketing pages in `_raw` mention “MFA” while `user` is anonymous. Suppress: filter `sourcetype=splunk_web_access` with `uri_path` allow-list via lookup `nis2_splunk_ui_allowlist.csv`.
• **Tenable scan gaps after network freeze** — Distinguish: `first_found` stops advancing because scans paused, not because patches failed. Suppress: join `index=maintenance` or time-bound exception rows in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `maintenance_window_id`.
• **ITSI maintenance flag forgotten** — Distinguish: `is_service_in_maintenance=0` but operations declared outage in ticketing. Suppress: enrich with `lookup snow:task` for `u_outage_active` and `where u_outage_active!="true"`.
• **Small-office Splunk user bases** — Distinguish: `total_users` under ~20 makes MFA percentages jumpy. Suppress: add `where total_users>=50` except for hosts listed in lookup `nis2_micro_office_hosts.csv` with `suppress_mfa_kpi=true`.
• **Duplicate vulnerability records** — Distinguish: multiple `plugin_id` rows per asset from duplicate agents. Suppress: pre-stats `dedup host plugin_id` in a macro before SLA math.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.x+ or Splunk Cloud stack with roles allowed to read `index=_internal`, `index=vuln`, `index=itsi_summary`, and optional `index=grc` / `index=itsm` for adjacent panels.
• Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) installed where `itsi_summary` acceleration runs; verify KPI search jobs complete within a **15 minute** budget.
• Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060) writing `sourcetype=tenable:vuln` (or mapped alias) into `index=vuln`; confirm `first_found` parses as epoch.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) optional modular inputs REST polling `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy` every **300 seconds** if you extend the UC with policy effectiveness joins.
• RBAC: create `nis2_effectiveness_users` with `srchIndexesAllowed` matching the indexes above—avoid granting `admin` for auditors.
• HTTP Event Collector token dedicated to pentest exports if you later append `index=pentest` findings.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable Splunk Web access logs (`splunk_web_access`) on search heads you want sampled; theyalready land in `index=_internal`. Route vulnerability scanner output to `index=vuln` and apply `props.conf` `EVAL-first_found` if the vendor sends text timestamps. On ITSI, ensure services carry `health_score` and maintenance flags synced from CMDB. Document baseline targets (`target` field in SPL) in `nis2_kpi_targets.csv` for traceability.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the SPL from the `spl` field into Search, verify three appended branches return rows, then Save As > Report > `UC-22.2.9_effectiveness_dashboard_feed`. Schedule weekly with `earliest=-7d@d` for operations and monthly `earliest=-30d@mon` for governance. Optional alert: trigger when any `status=FAIL`.

```spl
| makeresults
| eval measure="MFA_Coverage"
| append [
    search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_web_access earliest=-24h
    | stats dc(user) as total_users dc(eval(if(match(_raw,"(?i)mfa|2fa|totp"),user,null()))) as mfa_users
    | eval measure="MFA_Coverage", pct=round(100*mfa_users/total_users,1), target=95
]
```

Understanding this SPL: each `append` adds an independent KPI branch; `where isnotnull(pct)` drops the initial scaffold row. Tune `target` per risk appetite.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare `measure=Patch_SLA_Compliance` to the Tenable console for the same asset sample; spot-check `Service_Availability` versus an ITSI glass table. For MFA, pick one day and compare `_internal` hit counts to identity vendor sign-in logs (outside Splunk) for directionally similar adoption.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Publish a Dashboard Studio or Simple XML board with three KPI tiles and a combined table. Wire drilldowns to ES Incident Review (Splunkbase 263) if failures correlate with notables. Add email/Webex actions only after **48 hours** of noise review.

Step 5 — Governance
Quarterly, export results to `index=audit_evidence` with field `nis2_clause=Art.21(2)(f)` and archive using `evidenceSigning`. Maintain an exception register CSV for accepted KPI misses with expiry dates; join that register before alerting.
grandmaExplanation: We check a few simple health scores—how fast serious software flaws get fixed, whether important services stay in good shape, and a rough sign that people are signing in the safer way—so we can show outsiders our protections actually work week to week.
exclusions: Does not determine legal applicability, complete regulator submissions, approve policy decisions, or replace counsel/competent-authority review.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.9` extracts, dashboard PNG/PDF exports, and `index=audit_evidence` payloads referencing `measure`, `pct`, and `status` for NIS2 effectiveness monitoring.

controlTest.positiveScenario: For a chosen week, lower the Tenable SLA branch by ingesting a test host with `severity=High`, `first_found` older than 45 days, and `state=Active`, then run the search: `Patch_SLA_Compliance` must show `status=FAIL` while other measures still calculate.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After remediating that fixture host and rerunning Tenable so `state` is Resolved, or adjusting `first_found` inside policy SLAs, rerun over `-30d`; expect zero FAIL rows when all branches meet targets.
